compositions.name: ElevenLabs Conversational AI
compositions.intent: Hosted real-time voice agent stack from ElevenLabs that wires an ASR model, a configurable LLM, a low-latency TTS voice and a proprietary turn-taking model into a single managed conversational loop.
compositions.description: ElevenLabs Conversational AI (rebranded Eleven Agents) is a hosted platform for building voice agents that listen, understand and act in real time across voice and chat. The pipeline pairs a fine-tuned ASR, the developer's choice of language model and a low-latency ElevenLabs TTS voice with a proprietary turn-taking model, and is extended via System Tools (built-in actions such as agent transfer, end call, language detection), Client Tools (browser/mobile actions) and Server Tools (HTTP calls to the developer's backend).
primary_use_cases: customer support voice agents across telephony and web
multilingual voice assistants spanning ASR and TTS languages
multi-agent workflows with hand-off to specialised ElevenLabs agents
integration of CRM, calendar and payment backends via server tools
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Hosted streaming voice loop: incoming audio is transcribed by a fine-tuned ASR, the user's selected LLM produces a response, ElevenLabs TTS streams audio back, and a proprietary turn-taking model drives endpointing and barge-in. Tool calls fan out via System / Client / Server tools; agent transfer is a system tool that hands the live conversation to another configured agent.

key_concepts.name: Agent platform
key_concepts.summary: ASR + LLM + TTS + turn-taking model packaged as one configurable agent.

key_concepts.name: System tools
key_concepts.summary: Built-in actions that mutate conversation state without external calls (agent transfer, end call, language detection).

key_concepts.name: Client tools
key_concepts.summary: Tools executed in the embedding browser or mobile app.

key_concepts.name: Server tools
key_concepts.summary: HTTP-call tools that reach external systems and APIs.

key_concepts.name: Agent transfer
key_concepts.summary: Hand off an ongoing conversation to another designated ElevenLabs agent.

key_concepts.name: Multilingual voices
key_concepts.summary: Voice library spanning 5k+ voices and 70+ languages used by the agent's TTS leg.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  caller[Caller audio] --> asr[Fine-tuned ASR]
  asr --> llm[Configured LLM]
  llm --> tools{Tool call?}
  tools -->|system| sys[System tool: transfer / end / language]
  tools -->|client| client[Client tool in browser/mobile]
  tools -->|server| server[Server tool HTTP call]
  sys --> route{Agent transfer?}
  route -->|yes| other[Other ElevenLabs agent]
  route -->|no| llm
  client --> llm
  server --> llm
  llm --> tts[ElevenLabs TTS]
  tts --> caller
  turn[Turn-taking model] -.endpointing.-> asr
  turn -.barge-in.-> tts

members.note: Agent-to-agent transfer is a documented system tool that moves the ongoing conversation to another designated ElevenLabs agent.

evidence.title: Eleven Agents — agent transfer
evidence.quote: Agent-to-agent transfer allows an ElevenLabs agent to hand off the ongoing conversation to another designated agent when specific conditions are met.

evidence.title: Eleven Agents — system tools
evidence.quote: System tools enable your assistant to update the internal state of a conversation

members.note: TTS leg ships 5k+ voices across 70+ languages; conversation page advertises real-time language detection and switching.

evidence.title: Eleven Agents — 70+ languages
evidence.quote: A low-latency Text to Speech (TTS) model across 5k+ voices and 70+ languages

evidence.title: ElevenLabs Conversational AI — language detection
evidence.quote: Real-time language detection and switching across 70+ languages. Agents serve global customers without transfers or delays.

members.note: Three explicit tool categories: System tools (in-conversation state), Client tools (browser/mobile), Server tools (HTTP).

evidence.title: Eleven Agents — tools overview
evidence.quote: Tools allow ElevenLabs agents to perform actions beyond generating text responses.

evidence.title: Eleven Agents — client tools
evidence.quote: Empower your assistant to trigger client-side operations

evidence.title: Eleven Agents — server tools
evidence.quote: Connect your assistant to external data & systems

members.note: Proprietary turn-taking model handles interruptions and turn boundaries; conversation-flow page exposes the knobs.

evidence.title: Eleven Agents — turn-taking model
evidence.quote: A proprietary turn-taking model

evidence.title: Eleven Agents — conversation flow
evidence.quote: Configure turn-taking, interruptions, and timeout settings

members.note: WebSocket protocol delivers typed real-time events between server and client — base64 audio chunks, transcripts, agent responses, interruption events, and character-level alignment data.

evidence.title: Eleven Agents — low-latency TTS
evidence.quote: A low-latency Text to Speech (TTS) model across 5k+ voices and 70+ languages

evidence.title: Eleven Agents WebSocket — real-time connection
evidence.quote: Establish a WebSocket connection for real-time conversations with an AI agent.

evidence.title: Eleven Agents WebSocket — alignment data
evidence.quote: Character-level timing data for the audio chunk, useful for synchronized text display or lip-syncing.

members.note: A single agent dynamically switches among several named voices within one conversation (multi-character/persona mode) and returns to its default base voice, overlaying register rather than splitting into separate agents.

evidence.title: Multi-voice support - ElevenLabs Documentation
evidence.quote: Multi-voice support allows your ElevenLabs agent to dynamically switch between different ElevenLabs voices during a single conversation.

alternatives.note: Open framework alternative to a hosted platform.
Peer hosted voice-agent platform with similar ASR+LLM+TTS shape.

references.title: ElevenLabs Conversational AI — product page
references.quote: Conversational AI uses natural language processing (NLP), large language models (LLMs), and machine learning to interpret what someone actually means.

compositions.name: Hume EVI
compositions.intent: Hosted speech-to-speech voice API from Hume AI that pairs an emotionally aware response model with a configurable supplemental LLM, measuring vocal prosody and adapting tone in real time.
compositions.description: EVI is Hume AI's real-time speech-to-speech interface. It streams measurements of the tune, rhythm and timbre of the user's voice, reacts with matching prosody, and remains interruptible at all times. EVI is configured through a Config object (system prompt, voice, supplemental LLM, tools) and can be supplemented with partner LLMs from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google or Fireworks. Tool use and built-in tools are first-class but parallel function calls are not yet supported. Chat Groups link sessions so a conversation can resume across disconnects.
primary_use_cases: emotionally aware voice agents across consumer and support apps
multilingual speech-to-speech experiences on EVI 4 / 4-mini
function-calling voice agents that hit external APIs
long-running conversations resumed via Chat Groups
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Hosted speech-to-speech loop over a WebSocket. Caller audio streams in; EVI emits prosody measurements, decides the response (optionally via a supplemental LLM), streams generated speech back, and is interruptible by design. Tool calls go out to the developer's backend except for Hume's built-in tools which it invokes itself. A Chat Group id can be passed to resume across reconnects.

key_concepts.name: EVI speech-to-speech
key_concepts.summary: Streaming voice loop that measures vocal prosody and matches tone.

key_concepts.name: Configuration
key_concepts.summary: EVI Config defines system behavior, voice and supplemental LLM.

key_concepts.name: Supplemental LLM
key_concepts.summary: Optional partner LLMs from Anthropic / OpenAI / Google / Fireworks plug into the loop.

key_concepts.name: Tool use
key_concepts.summary: Developer-defined functions and Hume built-in tools; no parallel calls.

key_concepts.name: Multilingual EVI 4-mini
key_concepts.summary: Languages: English, Japanese, Korean, Spanish, French, Portuguese, Italian, German, Russian, Hindi, Arabic.

key_concepts.name: Chat Groups
key_concepts.summary: Link related chats so a conversation persists across disconnects.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  user[User audio] --> ws[WebSocket]
  ws --> evi[EVI speech-to-speech]
  evi --> prosody[Prosody measurements]
  evi --> resp[Response generation]
  resp --> llm{Supplemental LLM?}
  llm -->|yes| partner[Anthropic / OpenAI / Google / Fireworks]
  llm -->|no| native[Native EVI response]
  partner --> tool{Tool call?}
  native --> tool
  tool -->|developer fn| backend[Developer function]
  tool -->|built-in| builtin[Hume built-in tool]
  tool -->|none| tts[Streaming TTS reply]
  backend --> resp
  builtin --> resp
  tts --> user
  interrupt[User interjects] -.barge-in.-> tts
  group[Chat Group id] -.resume.-> evi

members.note: Chat Groups bundle individual chats so a conversation can resume across reconnects; a chat_group_id connects each chat to its group.

evidence.title: EVI chat history — Chat Groups
evidence.quote: Chat Groups link related chats to maintain continuity across multiple sessions, allowing conversations to persist even when users disconnect and reconnect.

evidence.title: EVI chat history — resumed conversations
evidence.quote: By default, a new chat session creates a new chat group. If the session resumes a previous conversation, the new chat is added to the existing chat group, preserving the full interaction history and context across sessions.

members.note: EVI 4-mini ships an explicit multilingual roster spanning eleven languages.

evidence.title: EVI overview — supported languages
evidence.quote: EVI 4-mini supports English, Japanese, Korean, Spanish, French, Portuguese, Italian, German, Russian, Hindi, Arabic.

evidence.title: EVI overview — language list verified 2026-05-24
evidence.quote: English, Japanese, Korean, Spanish, French, Portuguese, Italian, German, Russian, Hindi, Arabic

members.note: Developer-defined function calls plus Hume built-in tools; explicit caveat that parallel calls are not supported.

evidence.title: EVI tool use — function calling
evidence.quote: EVI simplifies the integration of external APIs through function calling.

evidence.title: EVI tool use — built-in tools
evidence.quote: Hume also provides built-in tools that are natively integrated. This means that you don't need to define the function; EVI handles both determining when the function needs to be called and invoking it.

evidence.title: EVI tool use — no parallel calls
evidence.quote: Currently, EVI does not support parallel function calling. Only one function call can be processed at a time.

members.note: EVI is documented as always interruptible, stopping rapidly when the user interjects, then resuming with the right context.

evidence.title: EVI overview — always interruptible
evidence.quote: Always interruptible: Stops rapidly whenever users interject, listens, and responds with the right context.

evidence.title: EVI overview — resumes from where it left off
evidence.quote: Stops rapidly whenever users interject, listens, and responds with the right context based on where it left off.

members.note: EVI can be supplemented with configurable partner LLMs from multiple vendors; the language-model configuration page explicitly lists Claude, GPT, and Gemini as external options.

evidence.title: EVI overview — supplemental LLM
evidence.quote: Optionally supplemented with configurable partner APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Fireworks, and more).

evidence.title: EVI language-model — external LLM choice
evidence.quote: Developers may also choose from leading external language models such as Claude, GPT, Gemini, and many others.

evidence.title: EVI language-model — specify a model
evidence.quote: EVI supports specifying a language model for response generation during chat sessions.

alternatives.note: Peer hosted voice-agent platform with similar ASR+LLM+TTS shape.

references.title: EVI overview
references.quote: Provides streaming measurements of the tune, rhythm, and timbre of the user's speech.

references.title: EVI tool use
references.quote: Tools are resources that EVI uses to do things, like search the web or call external APIs.

compositions.name: LiveKit Agents
compositions.intent: Open-source realtime agent framework that lets a Python or Node.js process join a LiveKit room as a full participant, with an STT-LLM-TTS pipeline, turn detection, tool calling and worker-based job dispatch.
compositions.description: LiveKit Agents is a framework for building realtime voice, video and multimodal agents that operate as participants in LiveKit rooms. The agent code acts as a stateful bridge between AI models and users: it streams audio through an STT-LLM-TTS pipeline, runs a custom turn-detection model for lifelike conversation flow, handles interruptions, and exposes a function_tool decorator so any LLM can call tools (including forwarding calls to the frontend). Agents run as workers; the agent server boots a 'job' subprocess that joins each room. Plugins cover STT, LLM, TTS and Realtime APIs.
primary_use_cases: realtime voice agents over WebRTC and telephony
multi-modal assistants that hear, see and speak
multi-agent handoff over LiveKit rooms
outbound calling, transcription and realtime translation
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Worker-dispatched, room-scoped event loop. A LiveKit worker registers with the agent server; when a room is created the server dispatches a job, the job subprocess joins the room, instantiates an AgentSession with STT, LLM, TTS and turn-detection plugins, and runs the streaming pipeline. Tools defined with @function_tool are exposed to the LLM, including forwardable frontend tool calls.

key_concepts.name: Agent
key_concepts.summary: A realtime participant that runs on the server and bridges users with AI models.

key_concepts.name: STT-LLM-TTS pipeline
key_concepts.summary: Streaming audio pipeline with reliable turn detection and interruption handling.

key_concepts.name: Turn detection
key_concepts.summary: Custom transformer model that detects end-of-turn to reduce barge-ins.

key_concepts.name: @function_tool
key_concepts.summary: Decorator that exposes Python functions as LLM-callable tools, forwardable to frontend.

key_concepts.name: Worker / job
key_concepts.summary: The agent server boots a job subprocess per room with load balancing.

key_concepts.name: Plugins
key_concepts.summary: Mix-and-match STT, LLM, TTS and Realtime API integrations.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  user[User in LiveKit room] --> server[Agent server]
  server --> dispatch[Dispatch job]
  dispatch --> worker[Worker subprocess joins room]
  worker --> session[AgentSession]
  session --> stt[STT plugin]
  stt --> turn[Custom turn-detection model]
  turn --> llm[LLM plugin]
  llm --> tool{function_tool?}
  tool -->|yes| run[Run tool / forward to frontend]
  run --> llm
  tool -->|no| tts[TTS plugin]
  tts --> user
  session -.barge-in.-> tts

members.note: Plugins expose multilingual modes (e.g. Deepgram nova-3 language='multi'); realtime translation is a documented use case.

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents README — multilingual STT
evidence.quote: stt=inference.STT("deepgram/nova-3", language="multi")

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents — realtime translation
evidence.quote: Realtime translation: Translate conversations in realtime.

members.note: Pipeline ships reliable turn detection and interruption handling; custom transformer model detects end-of-turn.

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents — pipeline interruption
evidence.quote: streaming audio through an STT-LLM-TTS pipeline, reliable turn detection, handling interruptions

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents README — turn detection
evidence.quote: Uses a transformer model to detect when a user is done with their turn, helps to reduce interruptions.

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents — turn detection
evidence.quote: Turn detection is the process of determining when a user begins or ends their 'turn' in a conversation. This lets the agent know when to start listening and when to respond.

members.note: @function_tool decorator exposes tools to any LLM; tool calls can be forwarded to the frontend.

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents — tools
evidence.quote: Define tools that are compatible with any LLM, and even forward tool calls to your frontend.

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents — tools page
evidence.quote: Let your agents call external tools and more

members.note: Agent is a realtime participant whose code reacts to room events and audio frames as they arrive.

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents — realtime participants
evidence.quote: The Agents framework lets you add any Python or Node.js program to LiveKit rooms as full realtime participants.

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents — stateful realtime bridge
evidence.quote: Your agent code operates as a stateful, realtime bridge between powerful AI models and your users.

members.note: A handoff transfers session control from one agent to another; returning a different agent from a tool call triggers automatic handoff and adds an AgentHandoff item to the chat context.

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents — handoff definition
evidence.quote: A handoff transfers session control from one agent to another. You can return a different agent from within a tool call to hand off control automatically.

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents — agent transfer control
evidence.quote: An agent can transfer control to other agents when different logic or capabilities are required.

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents — handoff event in chat context
evidence.quote: When an agent handoff occurs, an AgentHandoff item is added to the chat context with properties including old_agent_id and new_agent_id.

members.note: Each room gets its own job subprocess; the agent server boots a job subprocess per room with load balancing.

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents — worker job model
evidence.quote: agent server boots a 'job' subprocess which joins the room.

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents — dispatch and load balancing
evidence.quote: Assign agents to rooms automatically or explicitly, with load balancing and high concurrency support.

members.note: First-class MCP support via MCPToolset passed to an agent's tools parameter; MCPServerHTTP for remote and MCPServerStdio for local subprocess servers.

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents — first-class MCP
evidence.quote: LiveKit Agents has first-class support for Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers.

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents — MCPToolset
evidence.quote: Wrap an MCP server in an `MCPToolset` and pass it to the agent's `tools` parameter

evidence.title: LiveKit Agents — MCP transports
evidence.quote: Use `MCPServerStdio` to launch a local MCP server process and communicate over stdin/stdout.

members.note: LiveKit's handoff pattern enforces escalation to a human automatically when compliance mandates or rule conditions are met, passing the receiving human full context rather than leaving the transfer to model discretion.

evidence.title: The Handoff Pattern for Voice Agents That Replaces IVR Menus | LiveKit
evidence.quote: In healthcare (HIPAA), finance (SOX, PCI), and government (FedRAMP), certain actions legally require human oversight. The handoff pattern enforces this automatically.

alternatives.note: Peer open Python framework for voice agents.

references.title: livekit/agents
references.quote: A framework for building realtime, programmable participants that run on servers.

references.title: LiveKit Agents docs
references.quote: Realtime framework for voice, video, and physical AI agents.

compositions.name: Pipecat
compositions.intent: Open-source Python framework for building real-time voice and multimodal conversational agents by composing frame processors into pipelines that orchestrate STT, LLM, TTS, transports and tools.
compositions.description: Pipecat is an open-source Python framework whose unit of design is a Pipeline of FrameProcessors that exchange Frames (audio, image, text, transcription, transport messages). Built-in services span STT (Deepgram, AssemblyAI, OpenAI Whisper, ...), LLMs (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, ...), TTS (ElevenLabs, Google, Azure, ...) and speech-to-speech models (OpenAI Realtime, Gemini Multimodal Live), plus parallel pipelines, function calling, user-input muting and turn-detection strategies. Pipecat Flows layers conversation flows; the Subagents framework adds multi-agent.
primary_use_cases: voice assistants and AI companions
multimodal interfaces over WebRTC
interactive storytelling and business agents
complex dialog systems with structured flows
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Asynchronous pipeline of frame processors. A transport feeds audio frames in; an STT service converts them to transcripts; an LLM service produces text or tool calls; a TTS service streams audio out via the transport. Control and system frames orchestrate lifecycle. Parallel pipelines fan out with synchronized inputs and outputs. Function-calling frames interleave with the conversation; user-input muting and turn-detection strategies suppress processing during bot responses.

key_concepts.name: Pipeline
key_concepts.summary: Composition of FrameProcessors; ParallelPipeline runs branches with synchronized I/O.

key_concepts.name: Frames
key_concepts.summary: Audio, image, text, transcription and transport message frames plus control/system frames.

key_concepts.name: FrameProcessor
key_concepts.summary: Unit of pipeline logic; users write custom processors for domain logic.

key_concepts.name: Services
key_concepts.summary: STT / TTS / LLM / speech-to-speech integrations (50+ providers).

key_concepts.name: Function calling
key_concepts.summary: LLM tool use within the voice pipeline.

key_concepts.name: Pipecat Flows
key_concepts.summary: Structured conversation-flow layer on top of pipelines.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  transport[Transport WebRTC/Daily/LiveKit] --> audioin[Audio frames in]
  audioin --> stt[STT FrameProcessor]
  stt --> ctx[Context frames]
  ctx --> llm[LLM FrameProcessor]
  llm --> fc{Function call?}
  fc -->|yes| tool[Tool FrameProcessor]
  tool --> llm
  fc -->|no| tts[TTS FrameProcessor]
  tts --> audioout[Audio frames out]
  audioout --> transport
  parallel[ParallelPipeline] -.synced I/O.-> llm
  mute[User input muting] -.turn detection.-> stt
  flows[Pipecat Flows] -.structured turns.-> llm

members.note: Multilingual capability is delegated to chosen STT/TTS/LLM services; framework itself is language-agnostic so any multilingual provider stack works. Pipecat docs do not document a multilingual-specific switch; the capability comes via providers (Deepgram nova-3 multi, Google STT multilingual). Honest limited — no first-party Pipecat quote about a multilingual mode.

evidence.title: Pipecat docs — language-agnostic pipeline
evidence.quote: Open source Python framework for building voice and multimodal AI pipelines. Orchestrate 100+ AI services with ultra-low latency.

members.note: User mute strategies suppress incoming user audio/transcriptions during critical bot operations; configurable interruption strategies gate barge-in.

evidence.title: Pipecat docs — User Input Muting (mute during function calls)
evidence.quote: Mute during function calls. Prevents users from speaking while the bot is processing external data requests.

evidence.title: Pipecat docs — User Input Muting (strict turn-taking)
evidence.quote: Mute whenever the bot is speaking. Creates a strict turn-taking conversation pattern.

evidence.title: Pipecat docs — interrupted frames discarded
evidence.quote: If a user interrupts (starts speaking while the bot is responding), any pending data frames are discarded so the new input can be handled immediately.

members.note: Function calling is a first-class pipeline feature; handlers registered via register_function with per-function timeout and cancel-on-interruption controls.

evidence.title: Pipecat docs — function calling definition
evidence.quote: Function calling allows LLMs to request information from external services and APIs during conversations.

evidence.title: Pipecat docs — register_function
evidence.quote: Register handlers for your functions using `register_function` or `register_direct_function`.

evidence.title: Pipecat docs — function call cancellation
evidence.quote: Function call is cancelled if user interrupts

members.note: Pipecat's central abstraction is a Pipeline of FrameProcessors that exchange Frames — a textbook pipes-and-filters topology.

evidence.title: Pipecat README — orchestration
evidence.quote: Orchestrate audio and video, AI services, different transports, and conversation pipelines effortlessly.

evidence.title: Pipecat docs — ParallelPipeline
evidence.quote: Run multiple pipeline branches in parallel, with synchronized inputs and outputs for complex flows

members.note: Pipeline is built on streaming frames; high-priority SystemFrames carry interruptions, user-input, errors and lifecycle events; ControlFrames signal boundaries and config changes.

evidence.title: Pipecat docs — SystemFrames
evidence.quote: SystemFrames are high-priority signals that must always be delivered: interruptions, user input, error notifications, and pipeline lifecycle events.

evidence.title: Pipecat docs — ControlFrames
evidence.quote: ControlFrames signal processing boundaries and configuration changes: response start/end markers, settings updates, and state transitions.

members.note: Frames are explicitly typed (TextFrame, LLMTextFrame, TranscriptionFrame, InterimTranscriptionFrame, OutputAudioRawFrame, TTSAudioRawFrame, OutputImageRawFrame) and stream through processors in guaranteed order.

evidence.title: Pipecat docs — DataFrames content
evidence.quote: DataFrames carry the main content flowing through a pipeline: audio chunks, text, images, transcriptions, and messages.

evidence.title: Pipecat docs — TranscriptionFrame
evidence.quote: A non-interim transcription result from an STT service: the service's best recognition of what the user said.

evidence.title: Pipecat docs — frame ordering
evidence.quote: **Frames are processed in guaranteed order within their processing lane**, even across ParallelPipelines.

members.note: Open-source voice-AI pipeline with provider-agnostic TTS/STT/realtime stages.

evidence.quote: Open-source voice-AI pipeline with provider-agnostic TTS/STT/realtime stages.

alternatives.note: Peer open framework for realtime voice agents.

references.title: pipecat-ai/pipecat
references.quote: Open-source Python framework for building real-time voice and multimodal conversational agents.

references.title: Pipecat docs
references.quote: Open source ecosystem for building voice and multimodal AI agents.

compositions.name: Retell AI
compositions.intent: Hosted platform for building, testing, deploying and monitoring AI phone agents, with single-prompt, multi-prompt and conversation-flow agent shapes, function calling and call/agent transfers.
compositions.description: Retell AI is a phone-agent platform. Agents can be built as a Single-prompt agent (one open prompt), a Multi-prompt agent (state machine of prompts with transition conditions), or a Conversation Flow agent (explicit node graph with transition conditions). Function Calling, custom functions and code tools let the agent perform real-world actions; Transfer Call and Transfer Agent nodes hand off to humans or to other Retell agents. The dashboard exposes per-language selection with explicit accuracy trade-offs across the supported speech-recognition and voice providers.
primary_use_cases: lead qualification voice agents over telephony
customer support and dispatch phone agents
receptionist agents with call transfer
multilingual phone agents with language selection
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Hosted phone-agent loop. The platform owns the STT, LLM orchestration, TTS and turn-taking layers; the developer authors agent behaviour as a Single-prompt, Multi-prompt (state machine of prompts with transition conditions) or Conversation Flow (node-and-transition graph) agent. Function calls and code tools execute external actions; Transfer Call hands off to humans and Transfer Agent hands off to another Retell agent.

key_concepts.name: Agent types
key_concepts.summary: Single-prompt, Multi-prompt (state machine) and Conversation Flow (node graph) agents.

key_concepts.name: Conversation Flow
key_concepts.summary: Node-and-transition graph; transitions decide which node fires next.

key_concepts.name: Function calling
key_concepts.summary: Custom functions, code tools and built-in functions for transfers and digits.

key_concepts.name: Transfer Agent
key_concepts.summary: Hand off the ongoing call to another Retell agent.

key_concepts.name: Multilingual selection
key_concepts.summary: Pick exact language combinations; multilingual mode trades accuracy.

key_concepts.name: Language constraints
key_concepts.summary: Language must be supported by both the voice provider and a speech-recognition provider.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  caller[Caller] --> retell[Retell hosted pipeline]
  retell --> kind{Agent type}
  kind -->|Single prompt| sp[Single prompt agent]
  kind -->|Multi prompt| mp[Multi prompt state machine]
  kind -->|Conversation Flow| cf[Node + transition graph]
  sp --> fc{Function call?}
  mp --> fc
  cf --> fc
  fc -->|custom fn| api[Backend API]
  fc -->|code tool| code[Code tool]
  fc -->|transfer call| human[Human via PSTN]
  fc -->|transfer agent| other[Other Retell agent]
  api --> retell
  code --> retell
  retell --> caller

members.note: Transfer Agent is a documented action that modularises tasks and re-uses specialised agents without traditional phone transfers; Conversation Flow has agent/call transfer nodes.

evidence.title: Retell docs — transfer agent
evidence.quote: Agent Transfer enables you to modularize tasks and re-use specialized agents without relying on traditional phone-based transfers.

evidence.title: Retell docs — conversation flow
evidence.quote: Every node defines a small set of logic, and the transition condition is used to determine which node to transition to.

members.note: Per-agent language picker with documented accuracy trade-offs; language must be supported by both the chosen voice and a speech-recognition provider.

evidence.title: Retell docs — multilingual
evidence.quote: Pick the specific languages your agent should support, and understand the accuracy trade-offs.

evidence.title: Retell docs — language provider compatibility
evidence.quote: The language you pick must be supported by both your chosen voice provider (for pronunciation) and at least one speech recognition provider (for transcription).

members.note: Function calling covers built-in actions (transfer, press digit, send SMS) and custom functions / code tools that call external APIs.

evidence.title: Retell docs — function calling intro
evidence.quote: Function calling transforms your AI agent from a conversational interface into an action-oriented assistant.

evidence.title: Retell docs — custom function
evidence.quote: Enable your AI agent to perform actions like transfers, bookings, and API integrations

members.note: Multi-prompt agents and Conversation Flow agents are state machines that route the live conversation between prompts/nodes by transition conditions.

evidence.title: Retell docs — conversation flow transitions
evidence.quote: Every node defines a small set of logic, and the transition condition is used to determine which node to transition to.

evidence.title: Retell docs — prompt overview
evidence.quote: Choose between single or multi-prompt structures to build AI agents

members.note: Transfer Call is a documented action for handing the live call off to a human via PSTN with warm/cold transfer modes.

evidence.title: Retell docs — function calling lists transfers
evidence.quote: Enable your AI agent to perform actions like transfers, bookings, and API integrations

evidence.title: Retell docs — transfer call use case
evidence.quote: It is common in call operation to transfer the call to another human agent or another AI agent.

evidence.title: Retell docs — warm transfer
evidence.quote: After the call is transferred to the destination number, the AI agent can attempt to detect if the other side is human, leave private messages that are not heard by user, do a three-way introduce, etc.

alternatives.note: Peer hosted voice / phone agent platform.

references.title: Retell docs — introduction
references.quote: Retell is a comprehensive platform for building, testing, deploying, and monitoring reliable AI phone agents.

references.title: Retell AI homepage
references.quote: LLM based, humanlike, voice-first conversational AI platform

compositions.name: Vapi
compositions.intent: Hosted voice AI platform that orchestrates a transcriber, model and voice provider into a phone-callable assistant, with squads for multi-assistant handoff, function-calling tools and multilingual voice agents.
compositions.description: Vapi is positioned as an orchestration layer over three pluggable modules — transcriber (STT), model (LLM) and voice (TTS) — plus a suite of real-time orchestration models for endpointing, interruption handling, backchanneling and emotion detection. Developers configure an Assistant, attach Tools (function calling, custom and code tools, transfer call), and optionally compose Squads where specialised assistants hand off to one another mid-call. Multilingual mode adds automatic language detection.
primary_use_cases: outbound and inbound phone agents
customer support and lead qualification at scale
appointment scheduling and reception
multi-assistant workflows via squads
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Hosted real-time pipeline. Caller audio flows into Vapi's transcriber; the LLM produces a response interleaved with tool calls; a chosen TTS speaks the result back; a suite of real-time models layered on top of STT/LLM/TTS handles endpointing, interruption, backchanneling and emotion. Squads compose multiple specialised assistants and route the live call between them via assistantDestinations.

key_concepts.name: Assistant
key_concepts.summary: Configurable agent with transcriber, model, voice and tools.

key_concepts.name: Orchestration layer
key_concepts.summary: Vapi sits over transcriber + model + voice and adds real-time models.

key_concepts.name: Squads
key_concepts.summary: Multiple specialised assistants that hand off mid-call.

key_concepts.name: Tools
key_concepts.summary: Function calling, custom tools, code tools and call transfer.

key_concepts.name: Multilingual
key_concepts.summary: Multilingual assistants with automatic language detection.

key_concepts.name: Voice pipeline configuration
key_concepts.summary: Endpointing, interruption handling, backchanneling, filler injection.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD
  caller[Caller] --> vapi[Vapi orchestration]
  vapi --> stt[Transcriber STT]
  stt --> llm[Model LLM]
  llm --> tool{Tool call?}
  tool -->|function| fn[Function / custom / code tool]
  tool -->|transfer| xfer[Transfer call]
  tool -->|none| tts[Voice TTS]
  fn --> llm
  tts --> caller
  rt[Real-time models: endpointing/interruption/backchannel/emotion] -.augment.-> stt
  rt -.augment.-> tts
  squad[Squad: assistantDestinations] -.routes.-> llm

members.note: Squads let calls hand off between specialised assistants via assistantDestinations.

evidence.title: Vapi docs — squads
evidence.quote: Squads let you break complex workflows into multiple specialized assistants that hand off to each other during a conversation.

evidence.title: Vapi docs — assistantDestinations
evidence.quote: assistantDestinations defines how to transfer the call to another assistant

members.note: Explicit multilingual mode with automatic language detection across the orchestrated STT/LLM/TTS stack; multiple transcriber providers expose multi/multilingual modes.

evidence.title: Vapi docs — multilingual
evidence.quote: Configure your voice assistant to communicate in multiple languages with automatic language detection, native voice quality, and cultural context awareness.

evidence.title: Vapi docs — multilingual transcriber settings
evidence.quote: Set `Multi` (Deepgram), `Multilingual` (Google), or choose the language you want

evidence.title: Vapi docs — multilingual seamless switching
evidence.quote: Switch languages seamlessly when the user changes languages

members.note: Four explicit tool types: Default (built-in), Custom (webhooks), Code (TypeScript on Vapi infra), Integration (Make/GHL).

evidence.title: Vapi docs — tools
evidence.quote: Tools allow your assistant to take actions beyond just conversation. They enable your assistant to perform tasks like transferring calls, accessing external data, or triggering actions in your application.

evidence.title: Vapi docs — Custom Tools
evidence.quote: Custom Tools: Your own functions that can be called by the assistant to interact with your systems via webhooks.

evidence.title: Vapi docs — Code Tools
evidence.quote: Code Tools: Write TypeScript code that executes directly on Vapi's infrastructure without setting up a server.

members.note: Voice pipeline configuration covers timing and interruption handling; orchestration models include interruption handling and backchanneling.

evidence.title: Vapi docs — voice pipeline configuration
evidence.quote: Complete guide to configuring VAPI's voice pipeline for optimal conversation timing and interruption handling

evidence.title: Vapi docs — real-time models
evidence.quote: On top of speech-to-text, a language model, and text-to-speech, we run a suite of real-time models that make conversations feel fast, fluid, and human.

members.note: Assistant config selects transcriber + LLM + voice independently; the three modules can be swapped with any provider (OpenAI, Groq, Deepgram, ElevenLabs, PlayHT, etc).

evidence.title: Vapi docs — orchestration over three modules
evidence.quote: At it's core, Vapi is an orchestration layer over three modules: the **transcriber**, the **model**, and the **voice**.

evidence.title: Vapi docs — swap any provider
evidence.quote: These three modules can be swapped out with **any provider** of your choosing; OpenAI, Groq, Deepgram, ElevenLabs, PlayHT, etc.

references.quote: Build voice AI agents that can make and receive phone calls

references.title: Vapi docs — how Vapi works
references.quote: Learn about the real-time models Vapi runs on top of STT, LLM, and TTS

compositions.name: Moshi (Kyutai)
compositions.intent: Full-duplex speech-text foundation model from Kyutai that models the user's and the agent's audio as two parallel streams, handling turn-taking natively in real time instead of via a separate voice-activity-detection pipeline.
compositions.description: Moshi (arXiv 2410.00037) is reported as the first real-time full-duplex spoken large language model, with roughly 200ms practical latency. It generates speech tokens from the residual quantizer of the Mimi streaming neural audio codec, models its own speech and the user's speech in parallel streams, and predicts text tokens (an inner monologue) corresponding to its own speech. This replaces the traditional cascade of voice-activity detection, speech recognition, dialogue, and text-to-speech with a single model.
primary_use_cases: real-time full-duplex voice conversation
native turn-taking without a separate VAD or endpointing pipeline
low-latency speech-to-speech dialogue
compositions.agent_loop_shape: Single full-duplex speech model rather than a cascade. Moshi continuously models two audio streams — its own speech and the user's — in parallel, predicting speech tokens from the Mimi codec plus a text inner monologue. Because both streams run continuously, the model decides when to speak or yield natively, without a separate voice-activity-detection or endpointing stage.

key_concepts.name: Two parallel audio streams
key_concepts.summary: Moshi models its own and the user's speech as two streams, enabling simultaneous speech and native turn-taking without separate VAD.

key_concepts.name: Mimi neural audio codec
key_concepts.summary: A streaming neural audio codec; Moshi generates speech tokens from its residual quantizer.

key_concepts.name: Inner monologue
key_concepts.summary: Moshi predicts text tokens corresponding to its own speech, which improves generation quality.

compositions.pattern_composition:
flowchart TD fw["Moshi (Kyutai)"] fw --> p1["Semantic Turn Endpointing
(first-class)"] fw --> p2["Unified Voice Interface
(supported)"]

references.title: Moshi: a speech-text foundation model for real-time dialogue

members.note: Moshi handles turn-taking natively via its dual-stream architecture instead of a silence-threshold VAD — the full-duplex realisation of this pattern.

evidence.title: kyutai-labs/moshi (README)
evidence.quote: Moshi models two streams of audio: one corresponds to Moshi speaking, and the other one to the user speaking.

members.note: A single speech-text model owns the whole speech loop (recognition, dialogue, and speech generation) rather than a cascade of separate providers.

evidence.title: Moshi: a speech-text foundation model for real-time dialogue
